steps.title: Spot the turning point
steps.content: The passage sets up a widely accepted model (the energy ladder) to explain fuel choice, then immediately signals disagreement with the word however and describes the model as reductive — indicating it oversimplifies the reality of household fuel decisions.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the answer
steps.content: Choice A directly identifies the passage's purpose: challenging a common explanation (the energy ladder model). The other options frame it differently — B treats it as a country comparison, C reverses the causal relationship, and D describes a model about prices instead of choice. That matches choice A — A is correct.
